answer:
Ahrefs API v3 utilizes two primary authentication methods: long-lived API keys and OAuth 2.0 [1][2][3].

For API keys, generate them in your Account settings and provide the key in the HTTP Authorization header [1][4]:
`Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`

For applications, Ahrefs Connect uses OAuth 2.0 with PKCE [3]. This flow requires authorizing the application via a redirect to Ahrefs, followed by a POST request to exchange the authorization code for an access token [3]. The access token is then used as a Bearer token in the Authorization header [3]. 

Public crawler endpoints do not require any authentication [5][6].

results.title: API keys creation and management | Ahrefs for Developers
